seo.title: Building Research Agents with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Research Agents with Vercel AI SDK enables powerful multi-step web search and synthesis capabilities directly in your Next.js apps. These agents fetch real-time data, analyze sources, and generate summaries, but face risks like hallucinations, unverified claims, and inconsistent tool outputs across LLM providers. Vex guardrails ensure source accuracy, claim validation, and bias detection for reliable research outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents typically start with a user query routed through Vercel AI SDK's generateText or streamText to an LLM like OpenAI or Anthropic. The agent uses tools for web search (e.g., Tavily or SerpAPI), fetches results, and iterates through analysis steps: source extraction, claim verification, recency checks, and synthesis. Responses stream back to React Server Components for real-time UI updates, with structured outputs ensuring consistent JSON for downstream processing.

The flow handles complex reasoning chains where partial tool results might lead to early hallucinations. Edge functions manage timeouts for long-running searches, while provider-specific formatting differences are normalized via SDK's useChat hook. Vex integrates as middleware to validate each step without blocking the streaming UX.

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Agents generate plausible but incorrect summaries mid-stream before full tool results arrive
risks.mitigation: Vex claim verification middleware pauses streaming until key facts validated

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Raw search snippets displayed without source credibility or recency checks
risks.mitigation: Vex source verification intercepts tool outputs before UI rendering

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: Different LLMs format JSON research results differently, breaking frontend parsing
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes structured outputs to consistent schemas across providers

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Multi-step research exceeds Vercel edge runtime limits (30s default)
risks.mitigation: Vex async verification offloads heavy checks, preserving streaming responsiveness
